title: hordeum jubatum var. boreale
intro: Hordeum jubatum var. boreale, also known as blue wildrye, is a perennial grass native to North America, characterized by its blue-green foliage and purple flowers. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant has been used to promote overall health and wellness, with benefits including reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and enhanced immune function. Blue wildrye can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or salve, and has also been used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory issues and as a natural diuretic.
